bids.reasoning: The opponent opened with the worst possible values for me (all valueF), which gives me very low utility. Since we're at t=0.00, I should open with my best offer (all my top-value choices: valueA for all issues). My max utility is 0.3*1.0 + 0.2*1.0 + 0.2*1.0 + 0.2*1.0 = 1.0. The opponent's opening is extremely aggressive/self-serving, so I'll counter with my ideal bid.

It's very early (t=0.02). The opponent has made two offers, both heavily favoring their position (all valueF values, with only a tiny concession on issueA from valueF to valueE). I should stay close to my top-utility bid. My best bid gives utility of 1.0 across all issues. I'll hold firm at my maximum utility bid since there's no pressure to concede yet, but I can slightly adjust one low-weight issue to signal willingness to negotiate.

We're very early in the negotiation (t=0.04). The opponent started with all valueF (worst for me) and has been slowly moving on issueA only (F→E→D), while holding firm on issueB, issueC, issueD at valueF. This is very slow concession. My top utility bid is all valueA. I should stay close to my ideal bid but can make a tiny concession to signal willingness to negotiate. Since issueA is my most important issue (0.3 weight), I'll hold firm there. I can slightly concede on a lower-weight issue - but at t=0.04 it's still very early, so I'll stay at my maximum utility bid.

It's very early (t=0.06), and the opponent is slowly conceding on issueA (F→E→D→C) while keeping issueB, issueC, issueD all at valueF (worst for me). My best offer is all valueA, giving utility = 0.3*1 + 0.2*1 + 0.2*1 + 0.2*1 = 1.0. Since we're at the very start, I should continue pushing my top bid to signal my preferences. I'll maintain my maximum utility offer.

The opponent is slowly conceding on issueA (valueF → valueE → valueD → valueC → valueB) but keeping issueB, issueC, issueD at valueF (worst for me). We're still very early (t=0.08), so I should stay near my top utility. I'll hold firm on issueA (valueA) and the other high-value choices, maintaining my opening bid since the opponent hasn't shown meaningful concessions on most issues yet.

The opponent has been conceding on issueA (moving from valueF to valueA) but holding firm on issueB, issueC, and issueD at valueF, which gives me very low utility on those issues. We're early (t=0.1), so I can still hold firm on my top bid. I'll maintain my maximum utility bid since the opponent is clearly still conceding and we have plenty of time left.

The opponent is slowly conceding on issueA (moved from valueF to valueA over 10 steps) but keeping issueB, issueC, issueD at their worst values (valueF) for me. They just moved issueD from valueF to valueE, a tiny concession. We're very early (t=0.12), so I can stay firm near my optimal bid. My top utility is all valueA's = 1.0. I'll hold firm on my best offer since there's no pressure to concede yet, but I can make a small gesture to show willingness to negotiate by slightly adjusting one low-weight issue.

The opponent has been gradually conceding on issueA (now at valueA, best for me) and slightly on issueB and issueD, while holding issueC at valueF. It's still very early (t=0.14), so I should maintain my high-utility bid. My max utility bid is all valueA across all issues. The opponent is slowly working their way toward better values for me, so I should stay firm and not concede yet.

My utility for this: 0.3*1.0 + 0.2*0.20 + 0.2*0.68 + 0.2*0.20 = 0.30 + 0.04 + 0.136 + 0.04 = 0.516. It's early (t=0.16), so I should still push for high utility. The opponent is conceding on issueA (now at valueA, best for me), issueB (still at valueE), issueC (now at valueD), and issueD (at valueF). They're slowly improving their offers. We're still early at t=0.18, so I can stay near my top utility bid. My best offer is all valueA's (utility = 1.0). The opponent is conceding on issueA but holding firm on issueB (still at valueE/F), issueC (at valueD/F), and issueD (at valueE/F). Their latest offer gives me utility: 0.3×1.0 + 0.2×0.20 + 0.2×0.71 + 0.2×0.20 = 0.30 + 0.04 + 0.142 + 0.04 = 0.522. That's still quite low. The opponent has been conceding steadily, improving issueA to valueA (best for me) and gradually improving other issues. Their latest offer (t=0.22) gives issueA: valueA, issueB: valueE, issueC: valueC, issueD: valueD. That utility for me is: 0.3*1.0 + 0.2*0.20 + 0.2*0.68 + 0.2*0.35 = 0.3 + 0.04 + 0.136 + 0.07 = 0.546.
